page: The UpCloud HIMEM-12xCPU-256GB is a memory-optimized virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor with 12 vCPUs and 12 physical cores clocked at 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared, and hyper-threading is supported. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a substantial memory-to-core ratio of 21.33 GB per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 500 GB SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU hardware is present.

Benchmark results indicate a clear tradeoff between single-core compute and memory capacity. The single-core CPU performance under stress-ng is poor, placing in the bottom 10%, which reflects the shared CPU allocation and modest 2.0 GHz clock speed. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth perform at average levels. Passmark memory scores are weak, but database operations and Redis SET operations maintain average performance, processing 16.82 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server provides high memory density without the premium of dedicated CPU cores, making it a viable option for memory-intensive applications that do not require continuous, high-performance compute. It is well-suited for large in-memory databases, caching layers, data buffering, and memory-heavy backend processing where CPU contention is acceptable.
description: The UpCloud HIMEM-12xCPU-256GB is a memory-optimized virtual server utilizing the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It is configured with an AMD EPYC 7542 processor offering 12 shared vCPUs at 2.0 GHz, paired with 256.0 GB of RAM and a 500 GB SSD. This configuration yields a high memory-to-core ratio of 21.33 GB per core. While single-core CPU performance is poor due to the shared CPU allocation, multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and Redis operations perform at average levels. This server offers a cost-effective profile for memory-heavy workloads that do not demand dedicated CPU resources. It is best suited for in-memory databases, caching systems, and memory-intensive data processing.

tagline: A memory-dense virtual server featuring 256 GB of RAM and shared AMD EPYC processors for memory-intensive caching and database workloads.

256 GB RAM providing 21.33 GB per core
Average multi-core and Redis database performance
Suited for in-memory databases and caching layers
